title: anodontea rupestris
intro: Anodontea rupestris, also known as the "Rock Anodontea" or "Coral Honeysuckle", is a flowering plant species in the family Malpighiaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. Anodontea rupestris has been traditionally used to improve respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate coughs, making it a valuable herb for various health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
